Basic analysis of the affects of n_points on overlaps and delta metrics

Joe DiRocco

April 10, 2026

Observations

It is made clear through the visualizations that as n_points increases so does the probability of an overlapping. Extreme outliers were observed more often as n_points increased leading to heavy skewness in the delta metrics. Our previous understanding of the overlap risk of these unfolding methods has held true. At n_points = 100 DFS has a 7% overlap rate, shortest_paths nearly 1%, and bfs with 0.4%.

Summary by n_points only

n_points trials overlap_prob mean_num_overlappings mean_min_delta median_min_delta mean_mean_delta median_mean_delta mean_median_delta
25 3000 0.0000000 0.0000000 0.0859666 0.0752094 44.74771 38.74276 13.03895
50 3000 0.0006667 0.0006667 0.0190333 0.0180264 111.11187 95.01238 28.09636
100 3000 0.0226667 0.0273333 -0.5725020 0.0038679 270.71441 208.83096 65.61492

Summary by method and n_points

method n_points trials overlap_prob mean_num_overlappings mean_min_delta median_min_delta mean_mean_delta median_mean_delta mean_median_delta median_median_delta neg_min_delta_prob
bfs 25 1000 0.000 0.000 0.1180170 0.1098945 37.99644 31.69434 13.34623 12.92526 0.000
bfs 50 1000 0.000 0.000 0.0336070 0.0299629 83.15138 73.91731 25.71530 25.03648 0.000
bfs 100 1000 0.003 0.003 -0.0428712 0.0078062 177.80481 161.69142 50.68663 50.00384 0.054
dfs 25 1000 0.000 0.000 0.0988152 0.0928012 52.55648 45.87324 15.16876 14.61533 0.000
dfs 50 1000 0.002 0.002 0.0134856 0.0222225 155.67352 139.67370 39.04278 37.98365 0.011
dfs 100 1000 0.056 0.070 -1.6731064 0.0041491 435.88090 396.33944 107.74345 104.85646 0.259
shortest_paths 25 1000 0.000 0.000 0.0410677 0.0356934 43.69022 38.55919 10.60186 10.30778 0.000
shortest_paths 50 1000 0.000 0.000 0.0100074 0.0083734 94.51070 86.41115 19.53100 18.99396 0.000
shortest_paths 100 1000 0.009 0.009 -0.0015286 0.0020770 198.45754 184.65324 38.41469 37.45705 0.016

Tail-risk comparison for min_delta

This compares the extreme lower tail (worst 5%) to a less-extreme tail band (15-20th percentile, labeled here as the 80-85% worst band) to show how sharp the drop becomes near the worst outcomes.

method n_points q05 q15 q20 band_80_85_mid tail_drop tail_drop_ratio
bfs 25 0.0279870 0.0528390 0.0643946 0.0586168 0.0306298 0.4774576
bfs 50 0.0071819 0.0138499 0.0157434 0.0147967 0.0076147 0.4853750
bfs 100 -0.0328809 0.0026876 0.0033795 0.0030336 0.0359145 -10.8390096
dfs 25 0.0226881 0.0432713 0.0512189 0.0472451 0.0245570 0.4802204
dfs 50 0.0042063 0.0092701 0.0111210 0.0101955 0.0059892 0.4125615
dfs 100 -8.6142755 -1.6281903 -0.4723885 -1.0502894 7.5639861 8.2018113
shortest_paths 25 0.0072099 0.0137977 0.0171950 0.0154964 0.0082865 0.4652622
shortest_paths 50 0.0015509 0.0030000 0.0036840 0.0033420 0.0017911 0.4640555
shortest_paths 100 0.0003028 0.0006683 0.0008381 0.0007532 0.0004504 0.4019788

Interpretation: q05 shows the worst-case behavior in the lower tail, while band_80_85_mid is a less extreme bad-case baseline. DFS has a sharp tail cliff and a small fraction of trials become much worse than the rest.

Visualizations

# Overlap probability by n_points and method
p_overlap_prob <- summary_by_method_n %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_points, y = overlap_prob, color = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.15)) +
  labs(
    title = "Overlap Probability vs n_points",
    x = "n_points",
    y = "P(num_overlappings > 0)",
    color = "method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_overlap_prob

# Mean number of overlaps by n_points and method
p_overlap_count <- summary_by_method_n %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_points, y = mean_num_overlappings, color = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Number of Overlappings vs n_points",
    x = "n_points",
    y = "Mean num_overlappings",
    color = "method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p_overlap_count)

# Delta trends (summaries)
delta_summary_long <- summary_by_method_n %>%
  select(method, n_points, mean_min_delta, mean_mean_delta, mean_median_delta) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(mean_min_delta, mean_mean_delta, mean_median_delta),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )

p_delta_summary <- delta_summary_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_points, y = value, color = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Delta Metrics vs n_points",
    x = "n_points",
    y = "Metric value",
    color = "method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_delta_summary

# Median-based delta trends
delta_median_long <- summary_by_method_n %>%
  select(method, n_points, median_min_delta, median_mean_delta, median_median_delta) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(median_min_delta, median_mean_delta, median_median_delta),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )

p_delta_median <- delta_median_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_points, y = value, color = method)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Median Delta Metrics vs n_points",
    x = "n_points",
    y = "Median value",
    color = "method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_delta_median

# Raw distributions for delta metrics
df_long <- df %>%
  select(method, n_points, min_delta, mean_delta, median_delta) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(min_delta, mean_delta, median_delta),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  )

p_delta_box <- df_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = factor(n_points), y = value, fill = method)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~metric, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Delta Metrics by n_points",
    x = "n_points",
    y = "Value",
    fill = "method"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

p_delta_box
